info.title: OnboardYou Config API
info.description:
REST API for managing OnboardYou ETL pipeline configurations.

Supports CRUD operations on pipeline configs stored in DynamoDB and 
dry-run validation of pipeline manifests via column propagation.

post.description:
Authenticate with email + password and receive Cognito tokens.
This endpoint does **not** require a JWT — it is the entry point
for obtaining one.

get.description:
List all pipeline configurations owned by the caller's organization.
The organization is resolved from the caller's JWT claims.

Retrieve the pipeline configuration for a given customer company.
The organization is resolved from the caller's JWT claims.

parameters.name: customer_company_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the customer company

put.description:
Update an existing pipeline configuration. Validates the pipeline manifest,
persists to DynamoDB, and updates the EventBridge schedule.
The organization is resolved from the caller's JWT claims.

parameters.name: customer_company_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the customer company

post.description:
Create a new pipeline configuration. Validates the pipeline manifest,
persists to DynamoDB, and creates an EventBridge schedule.
The organization is resolved from the caller's JWT claims.

parameters.name: customer_company_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the customer company

delete.description:
Delete a pipeline configuration and its associated EventBridge schedule.
The organization is resolved from the caller's JWT claims.

parameters.name: customer_company_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the customer company

post.description:
Returns a presigned PUT URL that the frontend uses to upload the CSV
directly to S3.  The S3 key is `{org_id}/{company_id}/{filename}`.

get.description:
Reads the CSV produced by the named `ShowData` pipeline step and returns
it as a structured JSON payload.  Returns 404 if the output file does not
yet exist (i.e. the pipeline has not run successfully with that step).

parameters.name: action_id
parameters.description: ID of the ShowData pipeline step

get.summary: List recent pipeline runs for a customer company.

post.summary: Trigger a pipeline run immediately via SQS.

409.description: A run is already in progress

get.summary: Get a single pipeline run by ID.

post.description:
For CSV files: reads the header row immediately and returns columns inline
(`status = "not_needed"`). For all other file types: validates the upload
exists and returns `status = "queued"` — the async Textract conversion will
run before the ETL pipeline executes.

Dry-run column propagation: parses the pipeline manifest, builds every
action, and folds `calculate_columns` through the chain. Returns the
column set after each step without executing any real transformations.

parameters.name: customer_company_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the customer company

requestBody.description: Pipeline configuration to validate (only the pipeline field is used)

200.description: Validation result with per-step column snapshots

400.description: Validation error — misconfigured action or schema conflict

get.description:
Retrieve the settings for the caller's organization.
The organization is resolved from the caller's JWT claims.

put.description:
Create or update the settings for the caller's organization.
The organization is resolved from the caller's JWT claims.

ActionConfig.description: Configuration for a single action in the pipeline

action_type.description: Factory key — selects the Rust implementation (e.g. `ActionType::GenericIngestionConnector`)

config.description:
Action-specific configuration (shape depends on action_type)

Deserialized into the concrete typed variant matching `action_type`
via the custom `Deserialize` impl below.

id.description: Unique identifier for this pipeline step

ActionConfigPayload.description:
Wrapper enum for action-specific config payloads.

Each variant holds the **concrete, typed** configuration struct for
its `ActionType`.  The `#[serde(untagged)]` attribute produces flat
JSON serialisation (no wrapper key), while `ToSchema` generates a
`oneOf` schema listing every config variant for OpenAPI.

ActionType.description:
All known action types in the pipeline.

This is the **single source of truth** — adding a new capability requires
adding a variant here, which then forces the factory to handle it
(exhaustive match). Serde maps these to/from `snake_case` JSON strings.

| JSON value                | Variant                |
|---------------------------|------------------------|
| `"workday_hris_connector"`| `WorkdayHrisConnector` |
| `"sage_hr_connector"`     | `SageHrConnector`      |
| `"scd_type_2"`            | `ScdType2`             |
| `"pii_masking"`           | `PiiMasking`           |
| `"identity_deduplicator"` | `IdentityDeduplicator` |
| `"regex_replace"`         | `RegexReplace`         |
| `"iso_country_sanitizer"` | `IsoCountrySanitizer`  |
| `"cellphone_sanitizer"`   | `CellphoneSanitizer`   |
| `"handle_diacritics"`     | `HandleDiacritics`     |
| `"rename_column"`         | `RenameColumn`         |
| `"drop_column"`           | `DropColumn`           |
| `"filter_by_value"`       | `FilterByValue`        |
| `"api_dispatcher"`        | `ApiDispatcher`        |
| `"show_data"`             | `ShowData`             |
| `"generic_ingestion_connector"` | `GenericIngestionConnector` |

oneOf.description: No auth / static bearer token / custom API key.
OAuth2 client credentials or authorization code flow.
Placeholder — resolved to a concrete type at runtime.

ApiDispatcherConfig.description:
Fully-typed API dispatcher configuration.

Uses `auth_type` as the discriminator field. The `Default` variant is a meta-type resolved to the organisation's stored settings at runtime.

BearerPlacement.description:
How the credential is attached to outbound requests.

This is a pure discriminator — the actual header name or query-param key
lives in [`BearerRepoConfig::placement_key`].

BearerRepoConfig.description:
Configuration for the bearer / API-key strategy.

# JSON config (manifest)

```json
{
    "destination_url": "https://api.customer.com/employees",
    "auth_type": "bearer",
    "token": "sk-live-abc123",
    "placement": "authorization_header"
}
```

placement.description: Where to place the token on the request.

placement_key.description:
Header name or query-param key (used with `CustomHeader` / `QueryParam`).
Defaults to `"X-API-Key"` for custom headers, `"api_key"` for query params.

token.description: The static token / API key. `None` means no authentication.

CellphoneSanitizerConfig.description:
Configuration for the cellphone sanitizer action.

| Field              | Type       | Description                                             |
|--------------------|------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| `phone_column`     | string     | Column containing the raw phone number                  |
| `country_columns`  | `[string]` | Priority-ordered country columns (ISO 2 or 3 values)   |
| `output_column`    | string     | Column to write the internationalised number into       |

country_columns.description:
Priority-ordered list of columns whose values are ISO 2/3 country
codes.  The first non-null value that resolves to a known calling
code wins.

output_column.description: Column to write the sanitised international number into.

phone_column.description: Column holding the raw phone number.

ColumnMask.description:
Configuration for a single column to mask.

# JSON shape

```json
{ "name": "ssn", "strategy": "redact", "keep_last": 4, "mask_prefix": "***-**-" }
```

| Field         | Type   | Default     | Description                             |
|---------------|--------|-------------|-----------------------------------------|
| `name`        | string | —           | Column name to mask                     |
| `strategy`    | string | `"redact"`  | `"redact"` or `"zero"`                 |
| `keep_last`   | int    | `4`         | Characters to preserve (redact only)    |
| `mask_prefix` | string | `"***-**-"` | Prefix replacing the redacted portion   |

ConfigRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /config/{id}` and `PUT /config/{id}`.

Clients send only the fields they own; the server stamps identity
and timestamps before persisting.

DedupConfig.description:
Configuration for the identity deduplicator.

Columns are inspected in priority order — the first non-null value across
the listed columns becomes the dedup key for that row.

# JSON config

```json
{
  "columns": ["national_id", "email"],
  "employee_id_column": "employee_id"
}
```

| Field                | Type     | Default                    | Description                                          |
|----------------------|----------|----------------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
| `columns`            | string[] | `["national_id", "email"]` | Columns to inspect for the dedup key (priority order) |
| `employee_id_column` | string   | `"employee_id"`            | Column whose value is used as the canonical ID        |

columns.description:
Columns to inspect (in priority order) when building the dedup key.
The first non-null value across these columns becomes the key.

employee_id_column.description: The column that holds the employee identifier (used for canonical_id).

DropConfig.description:
Configuration for the drop-column action.

# JSON config

```json
{
  "columns": ["col1", "col2"]
}
```

| Field     | Type          | Description                |
|-----------|---------------|----------------------------|
| `columns` | `[string]`    | List of column names to drop|

columns.description: List of column names to drop.

ErrorResponse.description: JSON error envelope returned to clients.

FilterByValueConfig.description:
Configuration for the filter-by-value action.

| Field    | Type   | Description                                             |
|----------|--------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| `column` | string | Target column whose values are tested against the regex |
| `pattern`| string | Regex pattern (Rust `regex` syntax); rows that match    |
|          |        | are **kept**, non-matching rows are dropped              |

GenericIngestionConnectorConfig.description:
Configuration for the `GenericIngestionConnector` pipeline action.

Accepts **any file type** (PDF, XML, CSV, images, etc.).  Non-CSV files are
converted to CSV asynchronously via AWS Textract before the ETL pipeline
runs.  The connector reads the pre-converted CSV from S3 using the same
`CSV_UPLOAD_BUCKET` as `CsvHrisConnector`.

# JSON config (user-facing)

```json
{
  "filename": "employees.pdf",
  "columns": ["employee_id", "first_name", "last_name"],
  "table_index": 0
}
```

| Field         | Type       | Required | Description                                                       |
|---------------|------------|----------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `filename`    | string     | **yes**  | Original upload filename — any extension supported               |
| `columns`     | [string]   | no       | Override column headers. If absent, the CSV header row is used    |
| `table_index` | number     | no       | Which Textract table to use (0-based, default 0). Only applies    |
|               |            |          | when a multi-table document is converted via Textract             |

columns.description:
User-defined column headers.

When provided, these override the header row in the converted CSV.
Must exactly match the number of columns produced by Textract (or the
CSV header count for native CSV uploads).  When `None`, column names
are taken directly from the first row of the CSV.

filename.description:
Original upload filename (e.g. `"employees.pdf"`, `"roster.xml"`).

The connector resolves the converted CSV path by stripping the file
extension and appending `.csv`:
`{org_id}/{company_id}/{stem}.csv` on `CSV_UPLOAD_BUCKET`.

table_index.description:
Zero-based index of the Textract table to extract.

Relevant only for multi-table documents (PDFs, etc.).  Defaults to `0`
(the first table found by Textract).  Ignored when the upload is
already a CSV — no Textract call is made for CSV files.

HandleDiacriticsConfig.description:
Configuration for the handle-diacritics action.

| Field           | Type      | Default   | Description                                          |
|-----------------|-----------|-----------|------------------------------------------------------|
| `columns`       | [string]  | `[]`      | Columns to transliterate                             |
| `output_suffix` | string?   | `null`    | Suffix for output columns; null = in-place replace   |

IsoCountrySanitizerConfig.description:
Configuration for the ISO country sanitizer action.

| Field           | Type   | Description                                          |
|-----------------|--------|------------------------------------------------------|
| `source_column` | string | Column containing the raw country value               |
| `output_column` | string | Column to write the normalised ISO code into          |
| `output_format` | string | `"alpha2"` or `"alpha3"`                              |

output_column.description: Column to write the normalised code to.

source_column.description: Column to read the raw country value from.

items.description:
The pipeline config as stored in the database and exchanged via the API.

```json
{
  "name": "Acme Onboarding Pipeline",
  "cron": "rate(1 hour)",
  "organizationId": "acme-corp",
  "customerCompanyId": "customer-123",
  "lastEdited": "2026-02-09T12:00:00Z",
  "pipeline": { "version": "1.0", "actions": [...] }
}
```

customerCompanyId.description: Unique identifier for the customer company (sort key)

lastEdited.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last edit — set by the server

organizationId.description: Unique identifier for the organization (partition key)

pipeline.description: The full ETL pipeline manifest (passed through to the ETL Lambda)

items.description: A pipeline run record as stored in the `pipeline_runs` table.

currentAction.description: Action ID currently being executed (updated during the run).

customerCompanyId.description: Customer company the pipeline ran for.

errorMessage.description: Error message if the run failed.

errorRow.description: Row index where the error occurred (if determinable).

finishedAt.description: When the run finished (ISO 8601), if it has.

oneOf.description: Snapshot of the manifest at the time of the run.
Validation result from the pre-run dry-run (steps + columns).

LoginResponse.description: Successful login response — Cognito token set.

id_token.description: JWT ID token (contains custom claims such as `organizationId`).

refresh_token.description: Refresh token (can be exchanged for new tokens).

oneOf.description: Keep the last N characters, replace prefix with `mask_prefix`.

Redact.description: Keep the last N characters, replace prefix with `mask_prefix`.

oneOf.description: Replace all values with zero (for numeric columns).

MaskStrategy.description:
The masking strategy for a single column.

| Strategy | JSON key   | Effect                                                    |
|----------|------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
| `Redact` | `"redact"` | Keeps the last N chars, replaces prefix with a mask string |
| `Zero`   | `"zero"`   | Replaces all numeric values with `0`                       |

OAuth2GrantType.description: The OAuth2 grant type this repo instance should use.

OAuth2RepoConfig.description:
Configuration for OAuth2-authenticated egress.

# JSON config (manifest)

```json
{
    "destination_url": "https://api.customer.com/v2/employees",
    "auth_type": "oauth2",
    "grant_type": "client_credentials",
    "client_id": "app-12345",
    "client_secret": "secret-value",
    "token_url": "https://auth.customer.com/oauth/token",
    "scopes": ["employees.write"]
}
```
```

OAuthRepoConfig.description:
Configuration for OAuth 1.0a signed requests.

# JSON config (manifest)

```json
{
    "destination_url": "https://legacy.customer.com/api/roster",
    "auth_type": "oauth",
    "consumer_key": "ck_abc123",
    "consumer_secret": "cs_secret",
    "access_token": "at_token",
    "token_secret": "ts_secret"
}
```
```

OrgSettings.description:
Per-organization settings stored in the database.

`default_auth` holds the full auth configuration that maps to a
concrete `ApiDispatcherConfig` variant (Bearer, OAuth, OAuth2).

**Bearer example:**
```json
{
  "organizationId": "acme-corp",
  "defaultAuth": {
    "auth_type": "bearer",
    "destination_url": "https://api.example.com/employees",
    "token": "sk-live-abc123"
  }
}
```

**OAuth2 example:**
```json
{
  "organizationId": "acme-corp",
  "defaultAuth": {
    "auth_type": "oauth2",
    "destination_url": "https://api.example.com/v2/employees",
    "client_id": "app-12345",
    "client_secret": "secret-value",
    "token_url": "https://auth.example.com/oauth/token",
    "scopes": ["employees.write"],
    "grant_type": "client_credentials"
  }
}
```

defaultAuth.description:
Full auth configuration — typed to `ApiDispatcherConfig`.

Must contain `"auth_type"` plus all fields required by the
chosen strategy (bearer, oauth, oauth2).

organizationId.description: Unique identifier for the organization (partition key)

PIIMaskingConfig.description:
Configuration for PII masking.

Accepts both the new `columns`-array format and the legacy boolean
format.  See module docs for examples.

PipelineConfig.description:
The pipeline config as stored in the database and exchanged via the API.

```json
{
  "name": "Acme Onboarding Pipeline",
  "cron": "rate(1 hour)",
  "organizationId": "acme-corp",
  "customerCompanyId": "customer-123",
  "lastEdited": "2026-02-09T12:00:00Z",
  "pipeline": { "version": "1.0", "actions": [...] }
}
```

customerCompanyId.description: Unique identifier for the customer company (sort key)

lastEdited.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last edit — set by the server

organizationId.description: Unique identifier for the organization (partition key)

pipeline.description: The full ETL pipeline manifest (passed through to the ETL Lambda)

PipelineRun.description: A pipeline run record as stored in the `pipeline_runs` table.

currentAction.description: Action ID currently being executed (updated during the run).

customerCompanyId.description: Customer company the pipeline ran for.

errorMessage.description: Error message if the run failed.

errorRow.description: Row index where the error occurred (if determinable).

finishedAt.description: When the run finished (ISO 8601), if it has.

oneOf.description: Snapshot of the manifest at the time of the run.
Validation result from the pre-run dry-run (steps + columns).

PipelineWarning.description:
A single warning emitted by an action during pipeline execution.

Warnings are non-fatal — the pipeline continues but the client
should be informed so they can fix their source data.

detail.description: Optional extra detail (e.g. the un-parseable values).

PresignedUploadResponse.description: Response payload for the presigned upload URL request.

key.description: The S3 object key (for reference — not needed by the frontend).

upload_url.description: Presigned PUT URL — the frontend uses this to upload the CSV directly.

RegexReplaceConfig.description:
Configuration for the regex-replace action.

| Field         | Type   | Description                                    |
|---------------|--------|------------------------------------------------|
| `column`      | string | Target column to apply the replacement to      |
| `pattern`     | string | Regex pattern (Rust `regex` syntax)            |
| `replacement` | string | Literal replacement for the matched substring  |

replacement.description: Literal replacement text (backreference syntax is **not** honoured).

SageHrApiResponse.description: Top-level response from the Sage HR `/api/employees` endpoint.

SageHrConfig.description:
Configuration for the Sage HR REST API connector.

# JSON config example

```json
{
  "subdomain": "acme",
  "api_token": "<your-sage-hr-api-token>",
  "include_team_history": true,
  "include_employment_status_history": true,
  "include_position_history": true
}
```

| Field                              | Type   | Required | Description                                         |
|------------------------------------|--------|----------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| `subdomain`                        | string | **yes**  | Sage HR subdomain (e.g. `acme` → `acme.sage.hr`)    |
| `api_token`                        | string | **yes**  | API token provided by the user                       |
| `include_team_history`             | bool   | no       | Include team history (default false)                 |
| `include_employment_status_history`| bool   | no       | Include employment status history (default false)    |
| `include_position_history`         | bool   | no       | Include position history (default false)             |

SageHrEmployee.description: A single employee record as returned by the Sage HR API.

SageHrMeta.description: Pagination metadata from the Sage HR API.

ScdType2Config.description:
Configuration for SCD Type 2 effective dating.

# JSON config

```json
{
  "entity_column": "employee_id",
  "date_column": "start_date"
}
```

| Field           | Type   | Default         | Description                                      |
|-----------------|--------|-----------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `entity_column` | string | `"employee_id"` | Column that identifies the entity (partition key) |
| `date_column`   | string | `"start_date"`  | Column holding the date used for versioning       |

date_column.description: The column that holds the date / timestamp to use for versioning.

entity_column.description: The column that identifies the entity (partitioning column).

ShowDataConfig.description:
Configuration for the `ShowData` pipeline action.

No user-facing fields are required — the S3 output key is derived at
runtime from the organisation, company and action IDs:
`{org_id}/{company_id}/outputs/{action_id}.csv`

Multiple `ShowData` steps in the same pipeline write to distinct files
because each action has a unique `id` within the manifest.

s3_key.description:
Resolved S3 object key for the output CSV — injected at runtime.

Not present in user-authored manifests; set by the pipeline engine
immediately before the actions are built.

ShowDataResponse.description: Response body for a single `ShowData` output file.

columns.description: Column names derived from the CSV header row.

rows.description: Rows as JSON objects (column name → string value).

StartConversionRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /config/{id}/start-conversion`.

Instructs the backend to convert the already-uploaded file to CSV.
CSV files are short-circuited — no conversion is needed and column names
are returned immediately.

filename.description: The filename of the already-uploaded file (e.g. `"employees.pdf"`).

table_index.description:
Zero-based index of the Textract table / Excel sheet / JSON array to
extract from multi-table documents.  Defaults to `0` when absent.

columns.description: Column names of the CSV (always present on success).

status.description:
`"not_needed"` — file was already a CSV; columns returned inline.
`"converted"`  — file was converted to CSV synchronously; columns returned.

StepValidation.description: Result of validating a single step in the pipeline.

TriggerRunResponse.description: Response returned when a run is triggered.

ValidationResult.description: Overall validation result for the entire pipeline.

final_columns.description: Final column set after the last step.

WorkdayConfig.description:
Configuration extracted from the manifest `ActionConfig.config` JSON.

# JSON config example

```json
{
  "tenant_url": "https://wd3-impl-services1.workday.com",
  "tenant_id": "acme_corp",
  "username": "ISU_Onboarding",
  "password": "env:WORKDAY_PASSWORD",
  "worker_count_limit": 200,
  "response_group": {
    "include_personal_information": true,
    "include_employment_information": true,
    "include_compensation": true,
    "include_organizations": true,
    "include_roles": false
  }
}
```

| Field                  | Type   | Required | Description                                         |
|------------------------|--------|----------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| `tenant_url`           | string | **yes**  | Workday tenant base URL                              |
| `tenant_id`            | string | **yes**  | Workday tenant identifier                            |
| `username`             | string | **yes**  | Integration System User (ISU) username               |
| `password`             | string | **yes**  | ISU password (prefix `env:` to read from env var)    |
| `worker_count_limit`   | u32    | no       | Max workers per page (default 200)                   |
| `response_group`       | object | no       | Sections to include in Get_Workers response          |

WorkdayResponseGroup.description: Controls which data sections are included in the `Get_Workers` response.

bearer.description: Cognito JWT — the `custom:organizationId` claim is extracted by the Lambda authorizer and injected into the request context.

tags.name: CSV Upload
tags.description: CSV file upload and column discovery

tags.name: Show Data
tags.description: View data output from ShowData pipeline steps
